nits: withTimeout does not cancel the in-flight adapter HTTP request; on repeated timeouts, connection/memory footprint can accumulate until the underlying fetch settles. Consider passing an AbortSignal into adapter.poll to actually cancel the request.
Test timing relies on real setTimeout with tight elapsed-time assertions (100ms timeout + <500ms, 80ms + <700ms). Vulnerable to CI flake under load; vi.useFakeTimers() or wider slack would harden it.

Worst-case sweep duration is POLL_BATCH_SIZE × ADAPTER_TIMEOUT_MS = 50 × 15s = 750s, which exceeds the Lambda's 5-min ceiling. If ~21+ rows time out in one sweep, the Lambda is killed mid-loop; the tentative lease (5 min) covers it but operator should be aware.

PollTimeoutError is presented to the inner catch as a generic adapter failure (good for counting), but the failure label/log doesn't distinguish timeout vs. real adapter throw — would aid debugging if the catch branched on `e instanceof PollTimeoutError`.
`p.catch(() => undefined)` is fire-and-forget; correct for suppression, but slightly clearer if assigned to a variable or commented as intentionally floating.
reviewer: opus-4.7 adversarial pass via Agent tool
